summary: Backend-only testing of Droplet Manager API (DigitalOcean proxy + Windows install script generator). All 22 pytest tests passed covering: service status, Windows versions listing (8 items with expected keys), Windows script generator (valid win11pro, win10ltsc dd mode, invalid version, short password, rdp_port out of range), token requirement on all /do/* proxy endpoints (GET + POST + DELETE), action allow-list validation (invalid action_type), rebuild image requirement, and DO-forwarded auth error with invalid token.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py does not create an AsyncClient per request pool — this is acceptable given thin proxy nature but could be optimised with a shared httpx.AsyncClient lifecycle for performance.

WINDOWS_VERSIONS contains hardcoded ISO URLs, some signed/time-limited (e.g. win11pro zerofs link with exp timestamp). These may expire and break the Windows install flow silently — consider adding a periodic health check or documenting expiry.
No rate-limiting or token-format validation on X-DO-Token; a malformed header is forwarded to DO. Low risk but could return clearer errors.
CORS_ORIGINS default is '*' with allow_credentials=True — this is accepted by FastAPI but violates CORS spec; browsers will reject. Consider using explicit origins when credentials are enabled.
No MongoDB usage in server.py despite MONGO_URL/DB_NAME being present in .env — dead config (not a bug, just noise).
test_credentials: Not applicable — no auth layer. Used dummy token 'dop_v1_dummy_token' for allow-list tests and 'dop_v1_invalid_token_for_test' for DO-forwarded 401 test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_droplet_manager.py fully covers DO proxy auth layer + Windows script generator. Frontend was NOT tested (explicit skip). If future testing needs real DO API calls, a real token must be provided via env; current tests use dummy tokens and assert the thin-proxy forwards 401 correctly.
